info.title: InkyPi API
info.description: HTTP API for the InkyPi e-ink display controller. Manage playlists, plugins, display refresh, settings, and system health.

servers.description: InkyPi device on LAN via mDNS

get.description: Returns CPU, memory, and disk usage percentages, disk free/total GB, and uptime in seconds. Fields are null when psutil is not installed.
Returns the last-known health status for each plugin instance, filtered to a configurable time window (default 24 hours).

200.description: Plugin health map keyed by plugin ID

get.description: Returns the running application version and the latest available release from GitHub. Use update_available to decide whether to trigger an update.
Returns recent log lines from the inkypi.service journal (or log file fallback). Rate-limited to prevent abuse.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: Look-back window in hours (default 24, capped at 168)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log lines to return (default 500, capped at 5000)

parameters.name: contains
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring filter applied to each log line

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Minimum log level filter (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL)

get.description: Returns the most recently rendered image as PNG. Supports conditional GET via If-Modified-Since for efficient polling.

parameters.name: If-Modified-Since
parameters.description: Return 304 Not Modified if image has not changed since this timestamp

200.description: PNG image of the current display

304.description: Not Modified — image unchanged since If-Modified-Since

post.summary: Advance to next plugin in playlist
post.description: Triggers an immediate display refresh, advancing to the next eligible plugin in the active playlist. Rate-limited to prevent rapid successive calls.

get.description: Returns timing and context information about the most recent display refresh.
Returns the HTML playlist management interface.

post.description: Creates a new named playlist with optional time window and cycle interval.

delete.description: Permanently removes a playlist and its associated plugin assignments.

parameters.name: playlist_name
parameters.description: Name of the playlist to delete

get.description: Returns the HTML history browser showing previously rendered images.
Returns storage statistics for the display history directory.
Returns an HTML page with the Swagger UI explorer, loaded from CDN and pointing at /api/openapi.json.
Returns this OpenAPI 3.0 specification as JSON.
Returns build metadata used by the diagnostics/settings UI.
Returns process uptime and, when available, host uptime.
Returns an empty object when nothing is scheduled, otherwise the next playlist/plugin tuple.
Returns aggregate p50/p95 timings for recent refreshes.
Returns run counts and average timings per plugin.
Returns recent refresh timing rows for the diagnostics dashboard.
Returns stage timing rows for a single refresh_id.
Returns system/app diagnostics used by the in-app status badge and support tooling.
Polls a job id returned by POST /update_now?async=1.

post.description: Persists the drag-and-drop plugin order shown on the dashboard.
Saves the main settings form used by the settings page.

get.description: Returns the plugin IDs currently isolated from scheduling.

post.description: Adds a plugin ID to the isolated set and returns the updated list.

delete.description: Removes a plugin ID from the isolated set and returns the updated list.

post.description: Resets selected runtime settings to safe defaults while preserving playlists and plugins.
Persists API keys from the settings API-key manager and returns which keys changed.
Removes a stored API key from the managed settings page.
Triggers the settings-page software update flow and returns the running state immediately.
Triggers rollback to the previous recorded version after a failed update.

put.description: Renames a playlist and updates its schedule or cycle override.
Saves the device-wide playlist cycle interval in minutes.

post.description: Persists drag-and-drop plugin ordering for one playlist.

post.summary: Display the next item in a playlist
post.description: Triggers an immediate display of the next eligible plugin instance for one playlist.
Pushes a previously rendered image from history back to the display.
Deletes a rendered history image and its sidecar metadata.
Deletes all stored history PNG and JSON files.

warning.description: Optional non-fatal warning surfaced by the UI

cpu_percent.description: CPU usage percentage (null when psutil unavailable)

disk_percent.description: Disk usage percentage for root filesystem

disk_free_gb.description: Free disk space in GB for root filesystem

disk_total_gb.description: Total disk capacity in GB for root filesystem

uptime_seconds.description: System uptime in seconds since last boot

items.description: Map of plugin_id to health info

latest.description: Latest available release tag (null if GitHub unavailable)

update_running.description: True if an update process is currently running

release_notes.description: Release notes from the latest GitHub release

truncated.description: True if results were capped by limit or response size guardrail

request_ms.description: Time spent on the plugin data request in ms

generate_ms.description: Time spent on image generation in ms

preprocess_ms.description: Time spent on image preprocessing in ms

display_ms.description: Time spent pushing image to the e-ink display in ms

start_time.description: Active window start time in HH:MM format

end_time.description: Active window end time in HH:MM format

cycle_minutes.description: Override cycle interval in minutes for this playlist

order.description: Full ordered list of registered plugin IDs.

interval.description: Positive integer encoded as form data.

last_update_failure.description: Most recent update failure details (if any).

BadRequest.description: Bad request — invalid parameters or missing required fields

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded — try again after a short delay
